entity.summary: Iranian centralized cryptocurrency exchange that suffered a politically motivated destructive hack in June 2025, began phased service recovery later that month, and continued operating afterward. On June 2, 2026, the U.S. Treasury's OFAC designated Nobitex under counterterrorism and Iran financial-sector sanctions authorities.
entity.notes: The entity is returned from limited to active because post-incident recovery reporting, current official API documentation, and the June 2026 Treasury action all describe continuing operations after the 2025 hack. Active is an operational classification only and does not reduce the significance of the OFAC designation or sanctions exposure for counterparties.

expected.summary: Iranian centralized cryptocurrency exchange. In June 2025 it was hit by a politically motivated hack attributed to Predatory Sparrow / Gonjeshke Darande, with roughly USD 90 million in crypto burned or destroyed and the website/app taken offline during incident review.
expected.notes: Do not mark dead yet. Reuters and other sources report a major hack and offline website/app state, but current evidence does not prove permanent shutdown, liquidation, or service death. Keep as limited unless a later closure or durable recovery marker is confirmed.

changes.summary: Iranian centralized cryptocurrency exchange that suffered a politically motivated destructive hack in June 2025, began phased service recovery later that month, and continued operating afterward. On June 2, 2026, the U.S. Treasury's OFAC designated Nobitex under counterterrorism and Iran financial-sector sanctions authorities.
changes.notes: The entity is returned from limited to active because post-incident recovery reporting, current official API documentation, and the June 2026 Treasury action all describe continuing operations after the 2025 hack. Active is an operational classification only and does not reduce the significance of the OFAC designation or sanctions exposure for counterparties.

events.title: Nobitex began phased restoration of withdrawal and trading services
events.description: Nobitex's recovery roadmap moved into a phased service-restoration stage from June 30, 2025, initially reopening withdrawal access for verified users and planning gradual restoration of deposits and trading after the June hack.
events.notes: This is a phased-recovery marker, not proof that every product and wallet function returned simultaneously.

events.title: OFAC designated Nobitex under U.S. sanctions authorities
events.description: The U.S. Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control designated Nobitex under Executive Orders 13224 and 13902, citing support for the IRGC and operation in Iran's financial sector and warning of sanctions exposure for certain counterparties.
events.notes: The designation materially affects sanctions and counterparty risk but does not by itself prove operational shutdown.

evidence.title: Nobitex Announces Phased Recovery After Security Breach
evidence.notes: Reports Nobitex's recovery roadmap and the planned June 30 phased restoration of withdrawal, deposit, and trading functions.

evidence.title: Nobitex API documentation
evidence.notes: Current first-party API documentation supports continued post-recovery exchange, account, market, deposit, and withdrawal functionality.

evidence.title: Economic Fury Targets Iran’s Largest Digital Asset Exchange for Terror Finance and Sanctions Evasion
evidence.notes: Treasury announcement describing the Nobitex designation, legal authorities, named leaders, and alleged conduct.

evidence.title: Counter Terrorism and Iran-related Designations - June 2, 2026
evidence.notes: Official sanctions-list update supporting the designation date and scope.
